summary: Carole Herder reflects on equine behavior and the psychology of horses as prey animals, exploring why horses vary in temperament and how their senses shape behavior.
Customer testimonial praising the Cavallo Simple Boot for barefoot horses on rough Nevada Great Basin terrain, noting quick fastening, sand resistance, and hoof protection without sores.
Carole Herder discusses losing and rebuilding confidence around horses after a fall or accident, and the role of trust between rider and horse.
Customer testimonial on the Cavallo saddle pad resolving rub marks on a Tennessee Walker, plus praise for Cavallo TREK boots on a Rocky Mountain horse.
Carole Herder shares ideas for playing with horses at liberty, using cones, balls, and obstacle courses to add variety and build trust.
Carole Herder announces the book 'Horse Tales for the Funny Bone,' a collection of 60 equine stories that funds Therapeutic Riding Centers.
Customer testimonial describing how Cavallo Trek Boots helped a mare named Lulu recover comfort and stability after pasture injuries and hoof rotation.
Carole Herder argues that knowledge empowers better hoof-care decisions, challenging the assumption that long-shod horses cannot transition to barefoot or hoof boots.
Customer testimonial on Cavallo Sport boots staying secure on a Rocky Mountain horse through deep mud and bogs on a 20+ mile trail ride.
Carole Herder offers a humorous list of signs you are 'horse crazy,' from keeping Cavallo hoof boots on the shoe rack to prioritizing hay over dinner.
Carole Herder explores trust as the foundation of the horse-human relationship, drawing on Joe Camp's view that knowledge, lifestyle, and trust come before respect.
Carole Herder promotes an ACTHA giveaway for a free trip to Australia, including airfare, accommodation, and VIP tickets to the Equine Lifestyle 2015 Festival.
